erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.

goo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gone Dau.
goose: noun. Any of various grazing waterfowl of the family Anatidae, which have feathers and webbed feet and are capable of flying, swimming, and
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.
gorge: noun. The front aspect of the neck; the outside of the throat.
gorse: noun. An evergreen shrub, of the genus Ulex, having thorns, spiny leaves, and yellow flowers.
gouge: noun. Senses relating to cutting tools.
grego: noun. A type of rough jacket with a hood.
grog: noun. An alcoholic beverage made with rum and water, especially that once issued to sailors of the Royal Navy.
grouse: noun. Any of various game birds of the subfamily Tetraoninae which inhabit temperate and subarctic regions of the Northern Hemisphere;
grue: verb. To be frightened; also, to shudder with fear; to quake, to tremble.
grues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of grue

ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.

rouge: adjective. Of a reddish pink colour.
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
rug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Roviana.
rugose: adjective. Having rugae or wrinkles, creases, ridges, or corrugation.
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.

sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.

urge: noun. A strong desire to do something.
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
